title: Dukes of Brabant

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Duke+of+Brabant%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Duke+of+Brabant%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Duke+of+Brabant%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Duke+of+Brabant%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2010)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *[hertog](/wiki/Herzog "Herzog") van Brabant*, [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *duc de Brabant*) was the ruler of the [Duchy of Brabant](/wiki/Duchy_of_Brabant "Duchy of Brabant") since 1183/1184. The title was created by the Holy Roman Emperor [Frederick Barbarossa](/wiki/Frederick_Barbarossa "Frederick Barbarossa") in favor of [Henry I](/wiki/Henry_I,_Duke_of_Brabant "Henry I, Duke of Brabant") of the [House of Reginar](/wiki/House_of_Reginar "House of Reginar"), son of [Godfrey III of Leuven](/wiki/Godfrey_III_of_Leuven "Godfrey III of Leuven") (who was duke of [Lower Lorraine](/wiki/Lower_Lorraine "Lower Lorraine") at that time). The Duchy of Brabant was a feudal elevation of the existing (since 1085/1086) title of [landgrave](/wiki/Landgrave "Landgrave") of Brabant. This was an Imperial fief which was assigned to Count [Henry III of Leuven](/wiki/Henry_III_of_Leuven "Henry III of Leuven") shortly after the death of the preceding count of Brabant, Herman II of Lotharingia (born 20 September 1085). Although the corresponding county was quite small (limited to the territory between the rivers [Senne](/wiki/Zenne "Zenne") and [Dender](/wiki/Dender "Dender")) its name was applied to the entire country under control of the dukes from the 13th century on. In 1190, after the death of Godfrey III, Henry I also became duke of Lotharingia. Formerly Lower Lotharingia, this title was now practically without territorial authority, but was borne by the later dukes of Brabant as an honorific title.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Dukes of Brabant](/w/index.php?title=Dukes_of_Brabant&redirect=no "Dukes of Brabant"))

Position of Belgian nobility

"Duchess of Brabant" redirects here. For the consorts of the dukes of Brabant, see [Duchess of Brabant (by marriage)](/wiki/Duchess_of_Brabant_(by_marriage) "Duchess of Brabant (by marriage)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/Coat_of_arms_of_the_Duchy_of_Brabant.svg/250px-Coat_of_arms_of_the_Duchy_of_Brabant.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Coat_of_arms_of_the_Duchy_of_Brabant.svg)

Coat of arms of the [Duchy of Brabant](/wiki/Duchy_of_Brabant "Duchy of Brabant").

The **Duke of Brabant** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *[hertog](/wiki/Herzog "Herzog") van Brabant*, [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *duc de Brabant*) was the ruler of the [Duchy of Brabant](/wiki/Duchy_of_Brabant "Duchy of Brabant") since 1183/1184. The title was created by the Holy Roman Emperor [Frederick Barbarossa](/wiki/Frederick_Barbarossa "Frederick Barbarossa") in favor of [Henry I](/wiki/Henry_I,_Duke_of_Brabant "Henry I, Duke of Brabant") of the [House of Reginar](/wiki/House_of_Reginar "House of Reginar"), son of [Godfrey III of Leuven](/wiki/Godfrey_III_of_Leuven "Godfrey III of Leuven") (who was duke of [Lower Lorraine](/wiki/Lower_Lorraine "Lower Lorraine") at that time). The Duchy of Brabant was a feudal elevation of the existing (since 1085/1086) title of [landgrave](/wiki/Landgrave "Landgrave") of Brabant. This was an Imperial fief which was assigned to Count [Henry III of Leuven](/wiki/Henry_III_of_Leuven "Henry III of Leuven") shortly after the death of the preceding count of Brabant, Herman II of Lotharingia (born 20 September 1085). Although the corresponding county was quite small (limited to the territory between the rivers [Senne](/wiki/Zenne "Zenne") and [Dender](/wiki/Dender "Dender")) its name was applied to the entire country under control of the dukes from the 13th century on. In 1190, after the death of Godfrey III, Henry I also became duke of Lotharingia. Formerly Lower Lotharingia, this title was now practically without territorial authority, but was borne by the later dukes of Brabant as an honorific title.

In 1288, the dukes of Brabant became also [dukes of Limburg](/wiki/Dukes_of_Limburg "Dukes of Limburg"). The title fell to the [dukes of Burgundy](/wiki/Dukes_of_Burgundy "Dukes of Burgundy") in 1430. Later on, it followed with the Burgundian inheritance until the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution"), although the northern part of the territory of Brabant was actually governed by the [United Provinces](/wiki/Dutch_Republic "Dutch Republic") during the 17th and 18th centuries (see [Generality Lands](/wiki/Generality_Lands "Generality Lands")).

Today, the title of duke or duchess of Brabant designates, since the Royal Decree of 16 December 1840 (amended in 1991), the [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent") to the [throne of Belgium](/wiki/Throne_of_Belgium "Throne of Belgium"). The current holder is [Princess Elisabeth](/wiki/Princess_Elisabeth,_Duchess_of_Brabant "Princess Elisabeth, Duchess of Brabant").

## [House of Reginar](/wiki/House_of_Reginar "House of Reginar") (House of Leuven)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Duke_of_Brabant&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: House of Reginar (House of Leuven)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Gules_a_fess_argent.svg/250px-Gules_a_fess_argent.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Gules_a_fess_argent.svg)

* [Henry III](/wiki/Henry_III_of_Leuven "Henry III of Leuven") (1085/1086–1095); already [Count of Leuven](/wiki/Counts_of_Leuven "Counts of Leuven") and Brussels from 1078.

* [Henry I](/wiki/Henry_I,_Duke_of_Brabant "Henry I, Duke of Brabant") (1190–1235); already Duke of Brabant from 1183/1184
* [Henry II](/wiki/Henry_II,_Duke_of_Brabant "Henry II, Duke of Brabant") (1235–1248)
* [Henry III](/wiki/Henry_III,_Duke_of_Brabant "Henry III, Duke of Brabant") (1248–1261)
* [Henry IV](/wiki/Henry_IV,_Duke_of_Brabant "Henry IV, Duke of Brabant") (1261–1267)

**[Dukes of Brabant](/wiki/Dukes_of_Brabant "Dukes of Brabant"), [Dukes of Lothier](/wiki/Dukes_of_Lothier "Dukes of Lothier") and [Dukes of Limburg](/wiki/Dukes_of_Limburg "Dukes of Limburg")**:

* [John I](/wiki/John_I,_Duke_of_Brabant "John I, Duke of Brabant") (1267–1294)

* [John III](/wiki/John_III,_Duke_of_Brabant "John III, Duke of Brabant") (1312–1355)

## House of [Burgundy](/wiki/House_of_Valois-Burgundy "House of Valois-Burgundy")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Duke_of_Brabant&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: House of Burgundy")]

**[Dukes of Brabant](/wiki/Dukes_of_Brabant "Dukes of Brabant"), [Dukes of Lothier](/wiki/Dukes_of_Lothier "Dukes of Lothier") and [Dukes of Limburg](/wiki/Dukes_of_Limburg "Dukes of Limburg")**:

* [Anthony, Duke of Brabant](/wiki/Anthony,_Duke_of_Brabant "Anthony, Duke of Brabant") (1406–1415) (inherited title from his aunt, Joanna)
* [John IV, Duke of Brabant](/wiki/John_IV,_Duke_of_Brabant "John IV, Duke of Brabant") (1415–1427)
* [Philip I *of Saint-Pol*](/wiki/Philip_I,_Duke_of_Brabant "Philip I, Duke of Brabant") (1427–1430)
* [Philip II *the Good*](/wiki/Philip_III,_Duke_of_Burgundy "Philip III, Duke of Burgundy") (1430–1467), member of the [House of Valois-Burgundy](/wiki/House_of_Valois-Burgundy "House of Valois-Burgundy").
* [Charles I *the Bold*](/wiki/Charles,_Duke_of_Burgundy "Charles, Duke of Burgundy") (1467–1477), member of the [House of Valois-Burgundy](/wiki/House_of_Valois-Burgundy "House of Valois-Burgundy").
* [Mary](/wiki/Mary_of_Burgundy "Mary of Burgundy") (1477–1482), member of the [House of Valois-Burgundy](/wiki/House_of_Valois-Burgundy "House of Valois-Burgundy").

## Dynastic context

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Duke_of_Brabant&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Dynastic context")]

### [House of Belgium](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Belgium "Monarchy of Belgium") ([House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/House_of_Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha") until 1920)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Duke_of_Brabant&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: House of Belgium (House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha until 1920)")]

In the current [Kingdom of Belgium](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Belgium "Kingdom of Belgium"), the title of "Duke of Brabant" has been revived as a [dynastic title](/wiki/Substantive_title "Substantive title") at the court, and is traditionally assigned to the oldest son or daughter of the sovereign (even though the province of [North Brabant](/wiki/North_Brabant "North Brabant"), part of the historical duchy, is now part of the Netherlands and has been so since 1648).

The oldest son or daughter automatically becomes the duke/duchess of Brabant when his/her father becomes King of the Belgians; there is no ceremony or formal oath that the new duke/duchess must pass. When the Duke of Brabant loses his father, or when he abdicates, his title goes to the next in line, and he becomes the new King of the Belgians. Unlike the office of King of the Belgians, the eldest child of the sovereign does not need to reach the age of 18 to become Duke of Brabant. However, if a Duke or Duchess of Brabant has not reached the age of 18, he or she cannot ascend to the throne of Belgium, as stipulated by the constitution.
When the prince or princess becomes duke of Brabant, this title takes precedence of the other titles: Prince or Princess of Belgium.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The last Duke of Brabant became King in 2013, and passed the title to his oldest child. The current Duchess, [Elisabeth](/wiki/Princess_Elisabeth,_Duchess_of_Brabant "Princess Elisabeth, Duchess of Brabant") was only 12 years old when she became duchess in 2013. The Dukes and Duchess of the [House of Belgium](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Belgium "Monarchy of Belgium") ([House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/House_of_Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha") until 1920) are as follows:

* [Leopold II](/wiki/Leopold_II_of_Belgium "Leopold II of Belgium") (1840–1865), king of the Belgians.
* [Leopold](/wiki/Prince_Leopold,_Duke_of_Brabant "Prince Leopold, Duke of Brabant"), son of Leopold II (1865–1869) – the only prince who died a Duke of Brabant.
* [Leopold III](/wiki/Leopold_III_of_Belgium "Leopold III of Belgium") (1909–1934), king of the Belgians.
* [Baudouin](/wiki/Baudouin_of_Belgium "Baudouin of Belgium") (1934–1951), king of the Belgians.
* [Philippe](/wiki/Philippe_of_Belgium "Philippe of Belgium") (1993–2013), king of the Belgians.
* [Elisabeth](/wiki/Princess_Elisabeth,_Duchess_of_Brabant "Princess Elisabeth, Duchess of Brabant") (2013–present), the heir apparent to the Belgian throne.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/Coat_of_arms_of_the_Duchess_of_Brabant.svg/330px-Coat_of_arms_of_the_Duchess_of_Brabant.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Coat_of_arms_of_the_Duchess_of_Brabant.svg)

Coat of arms of Elisabeth of Belgium, duchess of Brabant.

#### Legislation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Duke_of_Brabant&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Legislation")]

Article 1, alinea 2, of the Royal Decree of 16 December 1840 says, since an amendment in 2001 : "*The title of Duke of Brabant or of Duchess of Brabant will be worn, in future, by the Prince or the Princess, elder son or daughter of the King, and, if lacking, by the Prince or the Princess, elder son or daughter of the elder son or daughter of the King*"[[3]](#cite_note-3)
